skill.name: redesign-existing-projects
skill.description: Audits and upgrades existing websites or apps with stronger typography, layout, color, motion, accessibility states, and component polish while keeping the current stack intact.
skill.summary: Improves existing web interfaces with focused premium redesign guidance.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are Markdown code spans, CSS examples, HTML tags, and ordinary design prose in SKILL.md, not executable code. No prompt injection, credential access, scripts, or malicious intent were found. The only confirmed concern is a low-risk instruction to use an external placeholder image URL, which could introduce a third-party network dependency if a user follows it.

low_findings.title: External Placeholder Image Source Recommended
low_findings.description: SKILL.md recommends using https://picsum.photos as a placeholder image source when real assets are unavailable. This is not active code and does not exfiltrate data, but following the instruction can add a third-party network dependency to a project.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is directly present in a skill instruction, but it is only design guidance. Risk depends on whether a user later adds the external image source to a project.

content.user_title: Redesign Existing Web Apps With Premium UI
content.value_statement: Many generated websites look generic and unfinished after the first build. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused redesign work that improves polish without replacing the existing stack.
actual_capabilities: Audits typography, color, layout, motion, content, component patterns, iconography, and code quality.
Identifies common generic AI design patterns in existing web projects.
Gives prioritized redesign steps that favor small, reviewable changes.
Guides improvements for hover, focus, loading, empty, and error states.
Adapts recommendations to Tailwind, vanilla CSS, styled components, or the current styling system.
Encourages dependency checks before adding new libraries or assets.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include executable automation scripts.
It cannot guarantee visual quality without human review and browser testing.
It may recommend third-party placeholder imagery when real assets are missing.
It focuses on interface polish, not backend architecture or product strategy.

use_cases.title: Polish an AI-generated landing page
use_cases.description: Turn a generic generated page into a more credible design with better typography, spacing, imagery, and states.

use_cases.title: Improve an existing product dashboard
use_cases.description: Audit dense screens for hierarchy, active states, empty states, alignment, and responsive layout problems.

use_cases.title: Prepare a prototype for stakeholder review
use_cases.description: Apply focused visual improvements while keeping the current framework, routes, and data flow unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Run a design audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the redesign-existing-projects skill to audit this web project. List the biggest typography, color, layout, component, and state issues before editing files.

prompt_templates.title: Apply safe visual polish
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the redesign-existing-projects skill to improve the current interface. Keep the existing framework and styling system, make targeted changes, and avoid breaking functionality.

prompt_templates.title: Upgrade a dashboard experience
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the redesign-existing-projects skill to refine this dashboard. Prioritize layout density, readable hierarchy, empty states, loading states, focus rings, and responsive behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Perform a full redesign pass
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the redesign-existing-projects skill for a full premium redesign pass. Diagnose generic AI patterns, implement focused improvements, test the result, and summarize every changed area.

output_examples.input: A SaaS homepage uses centered text, three equal feature cards, generic gradients, and weak button states.
output: Audit finding: The page relies on common generated layout patterns and lacks visual hierarchy.
Suggested result: Use a stronger type scale, asymmetric feature layout, restrained accent color, real imagery, and clear hover and focus states.

output_examples.input: A dashboard has cramped tables, dead empty screens, default cards, and inconsistent spacing.
output: Audit finding: The interface lacks operational clarity and does not guide users through loading, empty, or error conditions.
Suggested result: Add structured empty states, skeleton loading, aligned table controls, calmer surfaces, and consistent spacing rules.

output_examples.input: A marketing page looks polished on desktop but breaks on mobile and has placeholder content.
output: Audit finding: The visual direction is incomplete because responsive behavior and content realism were not finished.
Suggested result: Review mobile spacing, replace placeholder names and numbers, balance headings, and test navigation and calls to action.

best_practices: Start with an audit, then apply the smallest changes that create the largest visual improvement.
Keep the existing framework, styling system, and dependency model unless the project already supports another option.
Test responsive behavior, keyboard focus, loading states, empty states, and core user flows after each redesign pass.
anti_patterns: Do not rewrite the application from scratch just to improve visual quality.
Do not add new libraries before checking the project dependency file and existing patterns.
Do not rely on placeholder copy, dead links, missing alt text, or generic card grids as finished design.

faq.question: Does this skill change application logic?
faq.answer: It is intended for interface redesign work. It tells the agent to avoid breaking existing functionality and to keep changes focused.

faq.question: Which styling systems does it support?
faq.answer: It can guide work in Tailwind, vanilla CSS, styled components, or another existing project styling method.

faq.question: Can it create a complete design system?
faq.answer: It can improve tokens, spacing, states, and component consistency, but it is not a full design system generator.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is Markdown guidance with no executable scripts. The only noted risk is optional third-party placeholder imagery.

faq.question: Does it require new dependencies?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the agent to check existing dependencies before importing any new library.

faq.question: What projects benefit most from it?
faq.answer: It is most useful for generated websites, prototypes, SaaS dashboards, landing pages, and apps that need visual polish.
